name: uc4-airplane
_comment: UC4 airplane dataspace — simulated in-flight experience. Four mutually-exclusive content modes selectable from the dataspace HUD: IMU attitude / music / video / spatial photos. Only one renders at a time; the others stay loaded in the scene graph but hidden + paused.

udm_devices._comment: Simulated airplane IMU. Backed by mock-join-server for the demo; the real-device swap is a single URL change in the manifest below.
Procedural music boombox. P5b stub — plays an 8-second I-V-vi-IV loop generated in code (no audio asset to ship). Spatialised via THREE.PositionalAudio on the camera's AudioListener.

In-cabin HLS streaming display. P5c stub: plays the Mux test stream (Big Buck Bunny). Treat the stream URL as an external resource — replace with a real cabin-IFE backend later by editing the manifest's serviceRef URL.

Spatial-photo gallery: Gaussian-splat viewer cycling through .compressed.ply files (converted from source .sog via `npm run convert:spatial`). Sourced from ../../ml-sharp/samples and copied into public/spatial/. Treat as a 'frame' device: the manifest configures which photos it cycles through.

_marks_note: All four marks load but only one renders at a time — the dataspace HUD below switches via `show-only:<id>` actions. IMU is the default visible mode on entry; the others start hidden + (where applicable) paused.

config._comment_color: 0x4ec5ff — bright cyan-blue, matches boombox accentColor + globe atmosphereColor. Pairs with wireframe:true in liveImuCell.ts.

config._comment_size: 0.115 = 15% larger than the earlier 0.10 default — user feedback was the boombox felt too small on the centered stage.

marks.title: Cabin display
marks.subtitle: in-flight feature · 16:9
marks._comment_video: Back to HLS. The progressive-MP4 detour was a wrong turn: hls.js DOES work on Spectacles (240/480/720 renditions verified against the hls.js demo). The real problem was rendition height — the decoder chokes on 1080p+. videoPanel's initHLS now caps the ABR ladder at 720p (HLS_MAX_HEIGHT), which keeps Spectacles inside what it can decode while desktop/Quest still look sharp. The Mux x36xhzz stream is the hls.js demo's own default multi-bitrate Big Buck Bunny ladder.

marks.title: Travel Destinations
marks.subtitle: 3D Gaussian splats  -  ArrowL / ArrowR to navigate

photos.title: DSC 1624
photos.description: Walking through a moss-covered temple garden in late autumn. The stone path winds between cedar trunks centuries older than the shrine itself, with a small koi pond just out of frame to the right. Captured with a hand-held photogrammetry sweep at golden hour.

photos.title: DSC 8994
photos.description: A glacial-fed lake at first light, the surface a perfect mirror of the limestone ridge above. A pair of canoes are pulled up on the gravel shore. The mountain reflection makes the splat feel taller than it is — the algorithm captured both above and below the waterline.

photos.title: DSC 2913
photos.description: Late-afternoon souk light filters through woven palm-frond awnings onto stacks of spice cones — saffron yellow, paprika red, dried-rose pink. The narrow passage and overhead awning make the splat reconstruction read almost like a tunnel, with the spice colours saturating warmly along both walls.

config._comment_alpha_threshold: Climbed 5 → 20 → 35 → 60 as Photos-mode stayed fragment-bound. [perf] showed 2.33M tris/frame even at 35 — roughly ~1.1M splats per scene, which exceeds Quest 3's per-frame triangle budget at 90 Hz. 60 keeps only splats with alpha ≥ ~24% (60/255), expected to drop another 30-40% of splats. Visible quality cost: faint translucent splats (mist, hair, foliage edges) thin out; solid-surface fidelity unchanged. If Quest still struggles after this, the next lever is splatRenderMode:TWO_D in liveSplatGalleryCell.ts (2D billboards, 2-3× faster fragment shading, loses parallax-correct depth — fine for a stationary photo gallery). The clean-quality path is re-converting source .ply with splat-transform decimation flags.
config._comment_rotation: Quaternion [-1, 0, 0, 0] applies a 180° flip around X (horizontal mirror as viewed from the front). Per user feedback the SOGs were arriving mirrored.
